body:
Closes https://github.com/CliMA/Oceananigans.jl/issues/4416

I also changed the metric names from `immersed_boundary_mask` to `peripheral_nodes`, which is what we use in the code.
title: (0.96.23) Make `NetCDFWriter` work with `PartialCellBottom` grids

body:
In #4457 we forgot `Face` fields that do not allow `ValueBoundaryConditions`. This PR introduces an equivalent `PolarOpenBoundaryCondition` for fields with `LY == Face` (and a couple more tests).

for reference on main:
```julia
julia> grid = LatitudeLongitudeGrid(size=(10, 10, 10), latitude=(-90, 90), longitude=(-10, 10), z = (0, 1))
10×10×10 LatitudeLongitudeGrid{Float64, Bounded, Bounded, Bounded} on CPU with 3×3×3 halo and with precomputed metrics
├── longitude: Bounded  λ ∈ [-10.0, 10.0] regularly spaced with Δλ=2.0
├── latitude:  Bounded  φ ∈ [-90.0, 90.0] regularly spaced with Δφ=18.0
└── z:         Bounded  z ∈ [0.0, 1.0]    regularly spaced with Δz=0.1

julia> v = YFaceField(grid)
ERROR: ArgumentError: Cannot specify north boundary condition ValueBoundaryCondition: Oceananigans.BoundaryConditions.PolarValue{OffsetArrays.OffsetArray{Float64, 3, Array{Float64, 3}}, Symbol} on a field at Face()!
Stacktrace:
  [1] validate_boundary_condition_location(bc::BoundaryCondition{…}, loc::Face, side::Symbol)
    @ Oceananigans.Fields ~/development/Oceananigans.jl/src/Fields/field.jl:57

    @ Oceananigans.Fields ~/development/Oceananigans.jl/src/Fields/field.jl:101
  [7] Field(loc::Tuple{…}, grid::LatitudeLongitudeGrid{…}, T::DataType; indices::Tuple{…}, data::OffsetArrays.OffsetArray{…}, boundary_conditions::FieldBoundaryConditions{…}, operand::Nothing, status::Nothing)
```

title: Some more fixing for polar BC

body:
This might prove useful for building bathymetry on RectilinearGrids for a particular place on the Earth, using ClimaOcean's metadata features to automate the process. For example we would do something like

```julia
grid = RectilinearGrid(etc...)
corresponding_lat_lon = LatitudeLongitudeGrid(grid, origin=(210, 45))
bottom_height = ClimaOcean.regrid_bathymetry(corresponding_lat_lon)
ibg = ImmersedBoundaryGrid(grid, GridFittedBottom(bottom_height))
```

I could also see us building this into ClimaOcean's Metadata utilities directly, eg so we can also initialize T, S from datasets. 

cc @tomchor 

Also I just realized that we can construct the _exact_ grid for `OrthogonalSphericalShellGrid` (here, we have to make an approximation when computing the longitudes).

I think this is useful and we have the tests, so I'll put off the corresponding OrthogonalSphericalShellGrid implementation for a future PR.
title: Add a constructor for LatitudeLongitudeGrid from RectilinearGrid
